channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police in Georgia move again to disperse protests over the suspension of EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Police in Georgia's capital again moved to disperse thousands of demonstrators on Monday after over 200 people were detained during four previous nights of protests against the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Police say a Hawaii woman who disappeared went to Mexico and is not considered missing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Hawaii woman who disappeared after landing in Los Angeles three weeks ago was seen crossing into Mexico alone with her luggage and no longer is considered missing, police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Already buried under snow, Great Lakes region expected to see more stormy weather this week
item.description: Snow continued to fall Monday around parts of the Great Lakes region, where storm-weary residents who have plowed and shoveled for days faced the prospect of even greater accumulations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: These are the worst cities for package theft in 2024
item.description: The holiday season, which has already seen a surge in online shopping, presents even more opportunities for package thieves.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KTLA 5

item.title: Protests lay bare a clash of values and interests as Georgia navigates a Russia-West standoff
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Georgia is convulsed by political upheaval again after its pro-Moscow government, fresh off parliamentary elections denounced as rigged by its critics, decided to suspend negotiations for the small former Soviet republic to join the European Union.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Prosecution makes closing argument in murder trial over stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Closing arguments began on Monday in the trial of a tech consultant in the 2023 stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee in San Francisco, an event that shocked the broader tech community whose members mourned the loss of an entrepreneur they called charismatic and kind.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wave of Democrats slam Biden's pardon of his son Hunter
item.description: While some Democrats extended a measure of sympathy, President Biden has taken heat on several fronts.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is 'lake-effect snow'? Warm air from large bodies of water is the key ingredient
item.description: When towns along the Great Lakes get buried in drifts of blowing snow, like several have over the past few days, weather experts start talking about the “lake effect." Lake-effect snow often occurs in relatively narrow bands that dump copious amounts of snow.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Coast Guard suspends its search for the crew of a capsized fishing boat in the Gulf of Alaska
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The search for five people believed to be aboard a fishing vessel that capsized in rough seas in the Gulf of Alaska has been suspended, the Coast Guard said Monday. The search lasted nearly a day and covered more than 108 square nautical miles (370 square kilometers).
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth forced out of vets groups due to drinking, misconduct: New Yorker
item.description: The incidents — relayed in a trail of documents and corroborated to The New Yorker by the accounts of former colleagues — took place prior to Hegseth becoming a full-time Fox News TV host in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Woman cuts boyfriend after going through his phone: Memphis Police
item.description: A woman is facing charges after Memphis Police say she cut her boyfriend after going through his phone.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, NewsNation

item.title: Chainsaw-wielding man killed by police at assisted living facility in Illinois
item.description: The man had gotten into the lobby of the building and started confronting residents with the chainsaw, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: GOP's small majority adds to challenge of tax reform
item.description: House Republicans are heading into the new Congress with a lofty set of tax reform goals, an ambitious agenda made more difficult by a razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: White House celebrates 'peace and light' with holiday décor
item.description: The idea behind the theme, according to the White House, is to encourage the more than 100,000 visitors who pass through the doors at 1600 Pennsylvania Ave. during Christmastime to "embrace the peace and light of the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Best Cyber Monday 2023 tablet deals that make great gifts
item.description: BestReviews is reader-supported and may earn an affiliate commission. Details. Score deals on tablets from Apple, Samsung and Amazon Cyber Monday is here, and one constant is that you can find major savings on tech like tablets, both online and in stores.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Luna, ABC 4

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Lake-effect snow blankets the Great Lakes as Thanksgiving travelers head home
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Lake-effect snow blanketed swaths of the U.S. as Thanksgiving travelers journeyed home this holiday weekend, with more snow expected to fall over the Great Lakes region through the week.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, CAROLYN THOMPSON and JONAH BRONSTEIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Texas troopers catch smuggler who allegedly carried 5-year-old girl across border
item.description: The Texas Department of Public Safety arrested a man on Friday for his alleged involvement in smuggling a 5-year-old girl across the Rio Grande from Mexico into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares steps down as carmaker continues struggle with slumping sales
item.description: Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares is stepping down after nearly four years in the top spot of the automaker, which owns car brands like Jeep, Citroën and Ram, amid an ongoing struggle with slumping sales.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Girl Scout saves dad days after learning CPR with her troop
item.description: Just days before Vada Carawan's father suffered a heart attack, the 11-year-old and her Girl Scout troop had learned CPR.
item.dc:creator: Deja Parker, ABC 4

item.title: Saturn's rings will 'disappear' next year: Here's why
item.description: Saturn is best-known for its rings. But, for a short time next year, you'll hardly be able to see them.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Trump taps Kash Patel for FBI director, an ally who would aid in his effort to upend law enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump says he will nominate Kash Patel to serve as FBI director, turning to a fierce ally to upend America’s premier law enforcement agency and rid the government of perceived “conspirators.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, KRON4

item.title: Hero Girl Scout saves dad after he suffered heart attack in Virginia
item.description: Vada Carawan's Virginia Beach Girl Scout troop had just learned CPR 11 days earlier, and in that moment last Saturday, with her father unresponsive and her dog upset, she channeled her energies into saving him.
item.dc:creator: Deja Parker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in Death Valley National Park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KTLA 5

item.title: Credit card debt is soaring – what to know about getting out of it
item.description: With credit card debt reaching an all-time high, now is the time for the most pertinent questions and answers surrounding a difficult reality many Americans are facing.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KTLA 5

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, WGN 9

item.title: Why your favorite catalogs are smaller this holiday season
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Honey, they shrunk the catalogs. While retailers hope to go big this holiday season, customers may notice that the printed gift guides arriving in their mailboxes are smaller. Many of the millions of catalogs getting sent to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: The strike, dubbed "Make Amazon Pay," has planned demonstrations in more than 20 countries including the United States, and the protests are slated to last through Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Head of Ohio's teacher pension fund retires Sunday, saying fund is strong despite staff vacancies
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The outgoing head of Ohio's troubled teachers retirement system said that she is leaving the fund in strong fiscal condition, despite the turmoil at the top that her successor will inherit.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Colorado officer uses kid's bike during pursuit
item.description: A Lone Tree police officer went above and beyond to try and catch a theft suspect who fled on a scooter in a neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Bears fire head coach Matt Eberflus after disastrous Thanksgiving loss
item.description: The Chicago Bears have fired head coach Matt Eberflus following a six-game losing streak, with offensive coordinator Thomas Brown serving as interim coach.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Hot showers can harm skin, hair health, dermatologists say
item.description: Scalding showers can lead to the removal of skin layers and essential oils from your body, which can be damaging.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, Fox 5

item.title: 5 tips to avoid overspending this holiday season
item.description: Nearly 30% of credit card users are still paying off last year's holiday debt. Here are five tips to avoid overspending.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC
